title: Storm the Peaks — Ladakh & Nubra
excerpt: Dunes, monasteries, and the world's highest motorable pass. Cross into Leh, push north over Khardung La into Nubra Valley — sand dunes under snow peaks, Bactrian camels on the valley floor.

title: The Zanskar Expedition
excerpt: The valley the world forgot. Cross Shinku La into Lungnak valley, trek to Phugtal — a 12th-century monastery built into a cliff cave. Off-road, off-grid, unforgettable.

title: Full Ladakh Circuit
excerpt: Every pass, every lake, every road. Five high passes, three lakes, two desert valleys, and more monasteries than you can count. The grand lap of Ladakh.

title: Dome to Dome — Manali to Jispa
excerpt: The most cinematic 92km you will ever drive. Through the Atal Tunnel, past Sissu, along the Bhaga River — when the landscape changes completely on the other side.

title: Twin Lakes — Deepak Tal & Suraj Tal
excerpt: Two of the highest lakes in India. Suraj Tal at 4,950m — impossibly still, impossibly blue, fed by glacier meltwater. One day. Zero crowds.

title: Shinku La Pass — Snow Above the Clouds
excerpt: 5,100 metres. Snow year-round. No experience required. The closest major snow pass to YOLO Outdoors — prayer flags, glacier-level cold, and views that reframe everything.

title: Rohtang Pass — Snow at the Gateway
excerpt: The pass that divides two worlds. Lush Kullu Valley behind, stark Lahaul high desert ahead. Sledging, snowboarding, skiing — just 51km from Manali.

title: Beas River Rafting
excerpt: Grade II to IV rapids through the Kullu Valley. Pine forests, Himalayan peaks, and the clean cold of glacier-fed water rushing beneath you.

title: Solang Valley Adventures
excerpt: Snow scooters, paragliding, zorbing, skiing. Himachal's ultimate adventure playground against the backdrop of snow peaks. Manali as a playground.

title: Hampta Pass Trek
excerpt: Kullu forests to Lahaul desert in five days. The definitive Himachal crossover — two valleys, one crossing, and the optional Moon Lake of Chandratal.

title: Bhrigu Lake Hike
excerpt: A lake above the world. 4,300m altitude, wildflower meadows, and the sacred lake named after sage Bhrigu. High altitude, immediate reward.

title: Gumbok Rangan — The Sacred Peak of Lungnak
excerpt: A standalone sacred spire rising in isolation from the Lungnak Valley floor — one of the most dramatic mountain forms in the Himalayas. A full day from Jispa into the real heart of Ladakh.
